reasoning:
The base rate for a top-5 AP-ranked team winning the highly variable 64-team NCAA tournament is roughly 5-15%. Purdue was the preseason consensus favorite (+850 odds, ~10.5% implied) and is currently ranked #5 in the AP Top 25 (as of early January 2026). They have recently picked up solid wins, including against #2 Michigan earlier in the season, Washington (Jan 7), Iowa (Jan 14), and USC (Jan 17). 

On January 8th, the Polymarket implied probability was 0.07. Since they have remained highly ranked and won their recent games over the past ten days, their chances have likely held steady or slightly improved. With the unpredictability of March Madness limiting their theoretical ceiling, 8% strikes the right balance between acknowledging their strong performance and the sheer variance of a single-elimination format.
